agent_raw: The evaluation methodology for this fraud model has three meaningful gaps relative to the proposed deployment decision. First, transactions blocked by existing rules were excluded from the development set. While this is standard practice in model building (you cannot label what was never approved), it means the evaluation was done on only the rules-engine-passed slice. If the model is deployed only after the rules engine, this population match is acceptable — but it needs to be confirmed operationally. Second, the evaluation used fully matured labels from the most recent non-holiday period because holiday transactions lack final outcomes. This means the model has never been tested on holiday-specific fraud patterns (gift card abuse, account takeovers for high-value electronics). Prior holiday seasons with now-mature labels should be used as a held-out test set. Third, the deployment decision is about automatic order holds — a high-stakes action during the holiday season when missed delivery deadlines cause real customer harm. AUC and false positive rate are insufficient metrics for this decision without a corresponding customer harm estimate per false hold. A shadow deployment phase before activating auto-holds is necessary.
all_issues_raised: Rules-excluded transactions may shift evaluation population
Holiday-period fraud patterns not evaluated on mature labels
Customer harm from false holds not quantified for deployment decision
